applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused bias + double mish kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented Triton kernel that fuses bias addition and double mish activation. Fixed ROCm compatibility issues: replaced tl.libdevice.tanh with manual tanh using tl.math.exp ((exp(2x)-1)/(exp(2x)+1)), replaced tl.log/tl.exp with tl.math.log/tl.math.exp, added proper float32 casting. Uses BLOCK_SIZE=8192 (power of 2) for single-chunk processing per row.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile(mode='default')
attempted_but_failed.reason: Version incompatibility between ROCm torch and triton - ImportError: cannot import name 'specialize_impl' from 'triton.runtime.jit'. Also caused correctness issues.

attempted_but_failed.name: Smaller BLOCK_SIZE (1024, 2048, 4096)
attempted_but_failed.reason: No significant performance improvement - all block sizes yielded ~0.93-0.95ms runtime. The elementwise kernel is only 2.5% of total time.

notes: Initial Triton kernel implementation works correctly on ROCm (gfx950/MI355X)
Speedup is modest (1.036x) because GEMM dominates runtime (97.5%)
The elementwise kernel is already efficient at ~0.28ms per profile
Further optimization would require fusing matmul with elementwise ops
ROCm Triton requires tl.math.exp/log instead of tl.libdevice.* or bare tl.exp/log
torch.compile failed due to triton version incompatibility in this environment
Manual tanh implementation: clamp to [-10,10], then (exp(2x)-1)/(exp(2x)+1)
BLOCK_SIZE should be power of 2 and ideally match n_cols to avoid chunking overhead

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trials 2, 3, and 4 all failed (no output/timeout) attempting a custom Triton GEMM kernel. The hand-written GEMM approach is fundamentally flawed because rocBLAS is highly optimized for 1024x8192x8192

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 produced no output (timeout or crash), same as trials 2-4. The agent needs an extremely explicit code template. The key insight is to use torch.addmm (fuses bias+GEMM at BLAS level) and option
